cit.aids_levy_basis: The 3% AIDS levy is charged on the income tax amount itself (tax-on-tax), NOT on taxable profit.
cit.basis: 25% corporate income tax plus 3% AIDS levy on the tax, giving an effective rate of 25.75% (0.25 * 1.03).
cit.note: Administered by the Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA). Special mining lease and sector-specific rates are out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: Standard VAT rate of 15%; zero-rating and exemptions apply to certain basic goods and exports.

imtt.note: Intermediated Money Transfer Tax (IMTT) of 2% applies to most electronic/intermediated money transfers; caps and exemptions apply. Distinct rates may apply to foreign-currency transfers.

currency_regime.note: Zimbabwe operates a dual-currency regime: the Zimbabwe Gold (ZWG, introduced April 2024) alongside the US dollar. Tax may be assessed and payable in the currency of trade.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Sector-specific rates (e.g. special mining leases), capital allowances, relief, filing, and currency-of-assessment rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official ZIMRA guidance and a local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
